findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: 'where,' (3x).

findings.description: Professional engineer seal/stamp image is missing from the HTML conversion
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a North Carolina Professional Engineer seal (SEAL 038141) with signature. The HTML version on page 2 shows the seal image but it appears to be present. However, on page 1 (cover page) of the PDF, the seal is prominently displayed, while the HTML page 2 equivalent shows it.

findings.description: Cover page layout is significantly simplified in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 has a formal cover page with horizontal rule, large centered title block, and structured layout with prepared for/prepared by sections. HTML renders this as simple flowing text without the cover page structure.

findings.description: Repeated header content between page-equivalent sections in HTML
findings.evidence: The HTML includes repeated Geosyntec letterhead information (company name, license numbers) between content sections that correspond to different PDF pages, creating redundant content in the reading flow.

findings.description: Figure 2 flowchart image is missing from the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 13 shows a detailed flowchart (Figure 2) for determining concentration for loading at Outfall 002 with decision diamonds and process boxes. The HTML renders this as a text-based description of the flowchart logic rather than the actual flowchart image.

findings.description: Figure 3 flowchart image is missing from the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 14 shows a flowchart (Figure 3) for determining concentration for loading at the River Water Intake. The HTML renders this as text-based description rather than the actual flowchart image.

findings.description: Consent Order Addendum italic quoted text formatting partially lost
findings.evidence: PDF page 7 shows the Consent Order requirements in italicized block quote format with specific indentation structure. The HTML preserves the italic but the block quote structure with sub-indentation (c., i.) is slightly different.

findings.description: Mathematical equations rendered as plain text rather than formatted equations
findings.evidence: PDF pages 9-10 show Equations 1, 2, and 3 with proper mathematical notation including subscripts, summation symbols, and formatted variables. The HTML renders these as plain text (e.g., 'M_OF002 = Σ (n=1 to N) m_OF002,n - m_intake,n').

findings.description: The Consent Order Addendum sampling requirements text is rendered as an ordered list item (<ol type='i'>) with 'Outfall 002 Trendline:' as a list item, but in the PDF it is formatted as 'c. Outfall 002 Trendline:' with sub-item 'i.' — the 'c.' label is lost and the structural hierarchy is misrepresented.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'c. Outfall 002 Trendline: i. Starting no later than...' HTML uses '<ol type="i"><li>Outfall 002 Trendline:...' losing the 'c.' designation.

findings.description: The document title page content (prepared for, prepared by, project number, date) appears once in the HTML but the cover page content from the PDF page 1 is effectively rendered once, which is correct. However, the Table of Contents is reproduced in full as body text, which duplicates the section structure already present in the document headings.
findings.evidence: The full TOC with page numbers ('1 INTRODUCTION ................................................................................................ 1') is reproduced verbatim in the HTML body, which is acceptable as it mirrors the PDF.

findings.description: In the Equation 1 representation, the summation formula is rendered inaccurately. The PDF shows the subtraction inside the summation (sum of m_OF002,n minus m_intake,n), but the HTML text rendering is ambiguous and could be read differently.
findings.evidence: PDF equation: M_OF002 = Σ(n=1 to N) [m_OF002,n − m_intake,n]. HTML renders: 'M_OF002 = ∑ (n=1 to N) m_OF002,n - m_intake,n' — the lack of brackets makes it ambiguous whether m_intake,n is inside or outside the summation.

findings.description: Subscript notation in equations is rendered as plain text with underscores rather than proper subscript formatting, reducing readability but not technically altering the content meaning.
findings.evidence: PDF uses proper mathematical subscript notation (e.g., m_{OF002,j}). HTML uses 'm_OF002,j' as plain text.

findings.description: Table 1 accurately reproduces all 12 analyte rows with correct data. The 'Analytical Method' column correctly shows 'Table 3+ Lab SOP' for all rows. No data integrity issues found.
findings.evidence: All 12 PFAS compounds, their CASN numbers, chemical names, and formulas match between PDF and HTML.
